Assam · Northeast India · Community · Conservation

Rooted in Assam.
Working for the Northeast.

KOHUA is a community-led NGO from Assam, working across Northeast India for responsible tourism, conservation, art, music, and the living traditions of indigenous peoples.

scroll
What we stand for
Five pillars, one vision
🌿
01

Responsible Tourism

Ethical journeys honouring communities and landscapes of Northeast India.

🤝
02

Community

Empowering local voices, livelihoods, and leadership across the diverse Northeast.

🌳
03

Conservation

Protecting the extraordinary biodiversity and natural heritage of the region.

🎨
04

Art & Music

Sustaining rich traditions — weaving, craft, song, and performance.

🏺
05

Cultural Preservation

Documenting languages, oral histories, rituals and customs of indigenous peoples.

"The river remembers
what we forget."
Our Story
Born in Assam, belonging to the Northeast

KOHUA was born in Assam out of a deep love for Northeast India — its people, its forests, its songs, and its stories. We saw how fast-changing tourism and modernisation were reshaping indigenous communities, and we chose to act.

We work across the eight states of Northeast India with villages, artists, musicians, and storytellers to build a future where tradition and progress walk side by side.

8
States, One Region
40+
Artists Supported
500+
Lives Touched

Voices from the field
Stories from the ground
🌿
Field Report

How a village in Arunachal chose tourism over logging

March 2026 · Arunachal Pradesh

When the logging company came with offers, the elders of Ziro valley had another idea. KOHUA worked alongside them to build an alternative that preserved both livelihood and forest.

Read the full story → lass="story-card">
🧵
Community Voice

The last weaver of her village remembers

February 2026 · Assam

Ranjita Bora, 72, is the only person left who knows the full pattern of the traditional Mishing weave. We sat with her for three days, recording every thread of memory.

Read the full story →
🌳
Conservation

Children who became forest guardians in Meghalaya

January 2026 · Meghalaya

A KOHUA pilot with 40 schoolchildren in East Khasi Hills led to community-led protection of over 200 acres of sacred grove — driven entirely by the children themselves.

Rea
>
🎵
Music

Bringing a forgotten song back to its homeland

December 2025 · Nagaland

A folk song recorded in 1967 found its way home — back to the village in Nagaland where it was first sung. The reunion moved everyone to tears.

Read the full story →
